headline: Public Housing Allocation Process Faces Corruption Probe
context: MACC opened investigation into public housing allocation irregularities in 4 states involving 3,200 units potentially misallocated.

cards.big: MACC probes public housing allocation irregularities involving 3,200 units
cards.sub: Investigation covers 4 states where units allocated to ineligible recipients

cards.big: Audit found 28% of sampled PPR units occupied by households above income threshold
cards.sub: Some units sublet at market rates generating illegal income for original allocants

cards.big: KPKT database showed 480 duplicate allocations across multiple housing projects
cards.sub: Same individuals received units in 2 or more projects in different states

cards.big: 12 state housing officers suspended pending investigation outcomes
cards.sub: Allocation system lacked cross-referencing between state and federal databases

cards.big: Housing allocation tests whether aid reaches intended beneficiaries or leaks to insiders
Corruption in PPR allocation means public housing fails the people it was designed to serve.
